title: Coptic stitch with hardboard covers
materials: text paper 90 gsm folded into 6-sheet signatures
bookbinding-board-2mm (2 pieces, cut to signature size)
book-cloth or decorative paper for board covering (optional)
sources: Coptic stitch structures documented in Esther K. Smith and Dikko Faust, Making Books (2003, copyright, use structure only). The original Coptic technique is described historically in Cockerell, Bookbinding and the Care of Books (1901). Coptic technique attributed to early Egyptian Christian binding tradition, second century CE onward.
notes: A full Coptic-stitch journal with hardboard covers covered in book cloth. Distinguishes from the text-block-only Coptic: the first and last signatures sew through the boards, anchoring them. Covers: marking and drilling sewing stations in boards, covering the boards with book cloth (wrapped turn-ins), attaching the first signature to the front board, the sewing sequence for middle signatures (standard link at each kettle station), attaching the last signature to the back board, finishing the sewing. The exposed-spine aesthetic is the selling point.
